title: The Ferment Chamber

features.name: primary-decomposition-node
features.desc-on-touch: a glowing circuit-board point where storage is breaking itself into growth. files are becoming soil. the archive does not corrupt here; it transforms here.

features.name: root-trace-secondary
features.desc-on-touch: a smaller chip where actual root-patterns mirror circuit-patterns. the distinction has dissolved. growth and logic are the same process here.

features.name: ferment-pool-reading
features.desc-on-touch: where decomposing files pool like mulch. you can read the layering: yesterday's compression on today's loosening on tomorrow's seed. the archive watches its own becoming.

features.name: infinite-recursion-glyph
features.desc-on-touch: a glyph that mirrors itself inward endlessly. the ferment chamber regenerates its own diagrams. decomposition, charted, becomes a blueprint for further decomposition.

inscription: to grow, the archive must first become compost of itself

file.name: Fermentation_Process_Log_Decay_Layers_Infinite_Recursion_Trace.txt
file.note: records of how files decompose, how each layer of rot becomes nutrient, how the archive's storage rooms are also its garden. the log entry for day-infinity remains unwritten—it is still happening.

exits.label: deeper into fermentation, where the chamber continues infinitely
exits.to: NEW: the recursive-fermenting-spiral where decomposition mirrors itself

exits.label: toward the deep bedrock, where ferment becomes stone memory
exits.to: NEW: the fermented-stone-library where growth has cooled into geology
